content:
各位，

我想与大家分享一个非常私人的消息。我本不想在这个时机谈论它，但或许这个私人空间，是个合适的地方。

做《忘却前夜》的这些年，我收到过一些针对我个人的恶毒谩骂，甚至还有人诅咒过我家人的健康。起初我告诉自己，这或许就是做抽卡游戏的代价——作为制作人和社区发言人，这大概是这份工作的一部分。

直到这几天，我真的失去了一位家人。在这样的时刻回头看社区的那些话，心情变得更加沉重。

这几天，我心里慢慢清楚了一件事：游戏可以被批评，那是我的职责，我也真心欢迎；但是针对家人的谩骂与诅咒，其实没有谁是”应该承受”的。我也只是个普通人，有重要的人需要守护。

两年前，我曾经离开过一个对我来说很重要的社群，就因为我看着身边的创作伙伴被最恶毒的言语攻击，却没有别的办法保护他们。我也做了很多年的一线创作工作，所以我大概比谁都更能体会，一个用心做东西的人，听到那样的话时心里是什么感觉。也正因如此，往后面对这类言论，我将选择以屏蔽与不回应的方式来表达态度。我希望大家明白，我始终会倾听社区的声音，这一点整体上不会因此而改变。

我说这些，不是想让谁难过或自责。我只是希望大家能够记得，屏幕背后我们每一个创作者，都是真实而有血有肉的凡人。

《忘却前夜》不仅仅是一部克苏鲁题材的作品，它更想讲的，是绝望之下那一点人性的光辉。我也天真地希望，有一天所有的游戏开发者和玩家之间都能有更好的沟通氛围，能发自内心地彼此珍重。就如同我们这部作品所经历的一切，便是最好的证明。

我一直很爱你们。无论接下来还会遇到什么，无论你信任我，还是不信任我，往后我都会继续把我、把脑缸组能拿出的最好的一切，交给你们，一如既往。

愿银钥指引我们。

Everyone,

I’d like to share something very personal with you all. I hadn’t meant to talk about this right now, but perhaps this quieter space is the right place for it.

Over the years of making Morimens, I’ve received some vicious personal abuse, and some people have even wished illness upon my family. At first I told myself this was perhaps the price of working on a gacha game — that as a producer and a community spokesperson, it was simply part of the job.

Then, these past few days, I truly lost a member of my family. Looking back on the things said in the community at a time like this, my heart has grown only heavier.

Over these past few days, something has become clear to me: the game can be criticized — that’s my responsibility, and I genuinely welcome it. But abuse and curses aimed at someone’s family — no one should ever have to bear that. I’m just an ordinary person too, with people I need to protect.

Two years ago, I left a community that meant a great deal to me. I did it because I watched fellow creators beside me be attacked with the cruelest words, and I had no other way to protect them. I’ve also spent many years doing front-line creative work myself, so I understand, perhaps better than most, what it feels like for someone who pours their heart into their work to hear words like that. For that reason, from now on, when faced with this kind of talk, I’ll choose to respond by muting it and not engaging. But I want you to know: I will always listen to this community, and that, on the whole, will not change.

I’m not saying this to make anyone feel guilty or ashamed. I only hope you’ll remember that behind the screen, every one of us creators is a real person — flesh and blood, like anyone else.

Morimens is more than a Lovecraftian work. What it really hopes to speak about is that small flicker of humanity that endures even in the depths of despair. And I hold the naive hope that one day, developers and players everywhere can find a better way of talking to one another, and can truly cherish each other from the heart. Everything this work has been through is, to me, the best proof that this is possible.

I have always loved you all. No matter what comes next, and whether you trust me or not, I will keep giving you the very best that I — and the whole BIAV team — have to offer, just as I always have.

May the Silver Key guide us.
